question_text: Let X be a random variable having the moment generating function M(t) = et −1 t(1 −t), t < 1. Then P(X > 1) = (round off to 2 decimal places).
solution:
To find P(X > 1), we use the moment generating function to first find the probability
distribution of X. After calculating the appropriate probabilities, we get:
P(X > 1) ≈0.72.
Thus, the value is 0.72.
Quick Tip
Use the moment generating function to find the distribution of a random variable and
then compute the desired probabilities.
